picks.title: Nanocoder as an ACP agent: driving it from Zed
picks.why: The most substantive piece in the backlog: it explains what nanocoder --acp actually ships, how the editor-as-UI model differs from the VS Code extension, and what the agent boundary looks like in practice, which is the kind of architectural write-up a GitHub Discussion audience rewards.

Reddit rewards concrete before/after stories with a clear technical hook, and 33 tools collapsed to 19 plus an auto profile that picks itself from the model name is exactly that, especially for the small-local-model crowd that drives r/LocalLLaMA traffic.

A positioning story rather than a feature post: it explains why 'local-first CLI coding agent' was the wrong frame for the audience Nanocoder actually serves, giving LinkedIn readers a concrete take on collective-built software they can quote or push back on.

A sharp, self-contained hook for the small-model crowd: the auto profile infers full/minimal/nano from the model name and re-resolves live mid-session, which is the kind of one-liner X actually re-shares.
